play_store_reviews.text: Before using this app, I would struggle a lot with remembering things I had planned for the day, and it would always cause me to get into some trouble later because I just forget really important things. After a couple of days of using this app, I can definitely say a lot of stress has been taken off my shoulders. The only thing I have to remember now is to check the app daily, which is easy since you can set a specific notification sound for the tasks. Highly recommend this one 👌

The app is pretty much perfect for my needs, with this one exception: You can import Google calendars, but it doesn't show you the name of the Google calendars that it's importing, just the URLs. As a property manager, I import a lot of Google calendars, and I sometimes cannot distinguish one from the other based on the URL. I really, really, really need the name, and I hope they fix this problem soon because it's not a deal killer, but it's pretty close. If they can fix that, an instant 5-star

For YEARS I've been looking for a notes app that had everything I wanted. I am extremely forgetful and need my notes accessible. Good widgets, works without wifi, customizable, quick. I've tried Evernote, Google Keep, Color Note, OneNote, Samsung Notes, Etc. Nothing fit the bill. But I'm over a year into using TickTick and I will never look back. Bought premium after the first 3 months. This app works incredibly well and is a true life saver.

edit: Contacted support, they helped out very quickly. Yeah, give this app a shot! The app seems impressive. Many features are locked behind a pay wall, which is fine, but I was unable to do the free trial to decide whether I wanted to buy or not. I went through the process to start the free trial, and it didn't unlock anything and now just wants me to pay to keep using it, so... unfortunately that didn't fill me with enough confidence to pay to unlock anything.

Love this app! I've been using it everyday for over a year now to track my to-do list, my water intake, exercise, etc... It makes it so easy to keep track of things. No more scrolling Google play to find the right tracking app. I've got it now! I highly recommend the widgets too. There is a large number of templates you can use. You can stack them, change their transparency, and use multiple different widgets at the same time. Change the transparency, pop it on your home screen, easy access!

I use this as my master to do list. I have a notebook that has 4 lists: Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, and To Do. Each day I put a few items from my weekly list and maybe one from my monthly list on my daily to do list. At the end of the day I check them off of my Weekly list and they move to the bottom crossed out. It makes it super easy to see what still needs to be done. Its also super easy at the end of the week to un-check each weekly item to start the list off fresh for the next week.

have been using this app for more than 4 years now. as someone who's tried A LOT of task management systems, this is the one that's stuck as a straightforward yet comprehensive to do list ! I get all the functionality I need from the free tier, with calendar syncing, multiple lists (there is a limit on number of lists but I have big lists that are sectioned by topic, for example a list for school with sections by course), and widgets. it makes it easy to add tasks and check what's upcoming.

on the fence so far. the interface is fantastic, a superior one to Todoistv which was my go-to. on the other hand, I'm experiencing significant lag a few times a day, possibly sync related and I have to just sit and wait for it to come back. so far I'm about halfway through the trial and I really want to give this a thumbs-up and subscribe, but this issue does bother me. if anyone has any suggestions I'm quite open

Excellent - versatile and efficient Allows multiple levels of lists good for to do's, notes, habits, etc. Fast & simple - i.e. I have a list Shopping. When I open it I have the following lists to choose from: Groceries, House stuff, my wish list, gift ideas for kids, gifts for friends and family, etc. Another list I have is Notes. Here is a list of hings I took extensive notes on i.e. titles of books, philosophies I studied, nutrition/fitness strategies, etc. **with an easy search feature :-)

Update: Reached out to customer service via the app and they were great! Fixed my problem within 24 hrs! Great app, love it, has everything I need, but it will not work with my galaxy watch...I have Uninstaller, reinstalled, reset my watch, restarted my phone, checked all of the settings I was able to find on Google and YouTube to try and get it to work and nothing. I NEED the alerts or I will not remember to check the app and complete my tasks.

Wirecutter recommended TickTick and boy were they right. Its been wonderful. Categories, lists, reminders. Its the best to do list maker I've had so far. Yea! It's still going strong 4 years later. Superb app. Have never had a problem. Two more years and TickTick is rocking along. no failures, or anything negative. Often suggest it to others who are using all this fancy expensive stuff and get lost in the minutiae. Me? Daily, weekly, monthly, year and years ahead stuff all listed. Wonderful.

I honestly love almost everything about this app—it's very customizable and has a bunch of great tools/features. My one personal gripe with it is that there is no way to set when your day actually ends. I get off work late and am kind of a night owl, so often the most productive hours of my day run past midnight. it's a small inconvenience, but having to remember to switch to "yesterday" and possibly having to correct whatever mistakes that momentary lapse created is annoying.

Pretty good. Has all the right stuff. I was looking for a Google tasks replacement and this fits the bill. It has problems automatically syncing across devices so I always have to do a manual sync (or wait 20 minutes). Have checked the battery optimization is set to unrestricted. Still no good. Otherwise works well. Update: After messing with it and app support I'm unable to get it to sync with any regularly. Going back to Google tasks.

The best task tracking app I've found, I use it daily. I like that it isn't too complicated like a lot of team-oriented apps, but it lets me create lists of lists, there are a lot of different options for setting repeat tasks by completion and so on, I can also take notes, there's a nice calendar, and tags add another layer of organization so for example I can tag who will do the task or which tasks will be shown up on the family board. I also appreciate the many widgets available.

It's great it's this is all you use but it doesn't have an event option that just keeps appointments. It's all about things to do. They dissappear after the date. I need to be able to look back at when that happened. I also use google calendar. It was synced properly, but It constantly gave me double entries or didn't sync w google so i missed things. I tried all settings. Finally gave up. I don't have time to miss things. Good app, just not all I need.

Great functionality and relatively easy to use. The hardest thing for me is choosing which of the many ways to use it. Separate items into lists? Rely on tags? Use subtasks? Checklists? It has a lot of flexibility, which is why I've turned to it over Microsoft To Do or Google Tasks. Clickup was overwhelming and I didn't feel like I could get a good sense of whether Todoist worked without paying for it. If they could improve one thing, I think it would be more consistent UX across devices.

The "Complete" and "Snooze" buttons are still small, identical, & hard to see especially when asleep. Instead of useful improvements the look of snooze time set is now a circle that is annoying and difficult to use. hate that thing. it takes longer to fumble with until I give up. Let me tick off alarms without waiting for alarm to go off. i wish I never started with this because now I have to recreate all my alarms in a new app.

Have been using TickTick (free) for years. It mostly keeps me on track of my regular activities, and I like that I can schedule recurring activities a year or more out. I would like to see a category of Miscellaneous tasks that aren't necessarily pressing and don't have a due date but need doing at SOME point. I would also like to see a new task default to "today"; currently, if you don't assign it a date, it goes to the bottom of the list, so it may as well not exist.

12.22.25 update: loving it even more as I discover even more useful functions and, as a retired administrator, I'm only using the free version to organize my personal life. If I were back at administrating various groups and activities I would definitely purchase the pro version. I love the many options that tick tick gives me for any reminder. It is the best digital reminder system that I've ever used and I've been using them for forty years.

I don't use notifications, because they became spam or widgets. I use tasks, habits, and a sycned calendar. It would be nice to customize what gets notified. A little annoying that I have to use a separate calendar for appointments and events though it does sync properly to gmail. I don't appreciate when a product is provided for multiple platforms but only cares about one of them. Don't put out the new features for IOS until you have all OS coded for. Overall I like it, but some issues.

3.5 stars. Please give more sorting tools and control. I'd love to be able to put overdue things below current tasks, for instance, or have things sort so that the most recent due dates are at the top. The Kanban tool in desktop mode is the only reason I haven't given up on using it for my own to-do items, but I find the phone app frustrating and stressful. The reminders aren't clear if they're the "reminder" or the actual event (birthdays, e.g.). Ability to collaborate earns an extra star.

I've been using this app as my everything to do, task, reminders, small notes, habits/goals in the premium version for a few years now and there is no comparison in any of the others to all of the features provided (even free version) and the ability to customize. I am pretty OCD and particular about things, and there were always features missing from one or the other to do app that just never made any of them complete and able to streamline my life rather than complicate it. Best To Do app!

This app works like I think. You can group tasks and notes into folders and sub lists divided by headers. There are calendar views and a lot of appearance options, along with a wide variety of widgets and other features to try. I am having issues with a single recurring task that likes to skip a day at times. I would recommend this app to everyone.

I've tried lots of to-do apps and this one has all the functions I need. It has flexible snoozing options for alarms, persistent audible alarms and lots of cross-platform options, including in a web browser. You can back up, import or print tasks too, and view them with your calendar events to get a full picture of your day. And, of course, widgets. It even has a drag and drop Eisenhower matrix, something I've always wanred.

Best task app out there. Works well in free edition, but bought pro to help support a product I use every day. Also use the calendar support in pro edition, but it is not deal breaker. Keeps my tasks in sync and ready when i need them: paying bills on time, buying only what is on shipping list, etc. Five years after that review, still five stars. I've of the apps I use every day!

As someone who is picky about organization apps, this one has everything, such as a calendar, a to-do list within each calendar day, notes, folders, lists, changing the list layout, list categories, and tags. One of my favorite features is that this app adds an icon in your notification panel to quickly add a task without even opening the app (check all icons if you cant find it)! Total game changer for people who are worried about forgetting to use the app, and people who are always on the go!

Best to-do app on the market! (And I have tried many) My favorite feature is that if you have multiple lines of text, you just copy and paste it into the 'add' bar, and it asks you if you would like to make them multiple tasks! Furthermore, it automatically adds time information that you put in your tasks (like '5 pm - eat a vegetable), and this works with the aformentioned trick too. (5 pm - eat vegetable 6 pm - do a dance) So I just copy and paste my entire schedule from Onenote and there it is - that's usually all I need to do.

Best product for personal and small projects organization!. I was actually looking for a simple todo list, then I needed to unify all my calendars from different platforms, but also I wanted to save and follow up my ideas while keeping a journal, then I found myself trying to incorporate some good habits in my life, on the other hand, I found out that the pomodoro technique can really help me focus and get things done. Finally I found an elegant solution for all of this and more in TickTick.

This app is just what I needed. Checking a box gives off a little pleasant sound makes you feel good about sticking to a task, and gives you that little bit of motivation to do all the items on your list. Now I haven't forgotten anything thanks to this app. And I haven't even payed for the premium version. The free one includes the ability to link your Google Account to back up your data, and use it across multiple devices.

Please, this app is SO close to perfect. Please fix the shared list notifications, if you search reddit or anything you will see this has been an issue for a long while! Hopefully we can get better than a generic "we will forward this to our developer team" and actually get it fixed. It's the only thing stopping me from buying the premium membership, get it fixed and you will gain at least one customer, and I suspect more!

We just use this as a list management tool and ignore all the other features. It's perfect for shopping and Todo lists. My wife and I share an account so if one of us goes to the store the other can see the updated list and not risk duplication. Camping preparation lists, handyman lists, restaurants we want to try, etc., etc. We brought the premium version because we needed more lists. Great product.

Best to-do I've found, keep coming back to it even if I try something else. It has a lot of really useful features, while also not being overly complex, cluttered, or distracting. The free version has all the basic features, it is usable free. It is a shame they raised the subscription cost from $1.99/mo to $3.99/mo, that feels a bit excessive for a to-do app. I also wish they had a one-time purchase option, I hate subscriptions. That's the only reason I still look around for other options

There's a limit on how many tasks you can add, even on the paid version. I have the free version and can no longer add simple things like items to my grocery list -- those count as tasks. Overall, I really like TickTick and have used it for about 2 years. Sad to have to stop using it due to hitting the limit on adding to lists. Bummer.

This app really is a life saver. It can do so much more than you think it can! I use it as a To Do list (and other lists like bookmarks etc), I use it as a home management tool to remind me of all my home & life tasks like pet care, chores, bill pay reminders, & a ton more (using reoccurring tasks) I use it as a CRM even to manage when I need to keep in touch with specific friends/family (reoccurring tasks)! Project management, ETC. Best productivity tool I have ever used. & 100% worth premium!

I love this app! I can literally organize my entire life with this app and not worry about it. I make grocery lists, set reminders for appointments, create subtasks, and this isal all with the free version. If I need to make other arrangements I can do that with a few adjustments with the date and time. This is the first app I've seen in several years that may be worth the paid version. Just what I need!

I've used all of the popular and some of the lesser known To Do List apps. This is by far my favorite. You don't have to have an account and has more options. But you should for back up purposes. The only problem I have is that when I miss a task, it says due 10 minutes ago when it's been 2 hours. My tasks do show up on time though. The times vary, but this issue started when I created an account.

I've used a variety of To-do apps, in order to be more personally organized and productive, and ticktick has been the most engaging and successful for me. I don't need (or want) features that blend into team based task tracking, as I have plenty of options for those in my work life. This has been perfect for personal productivity, focus, habit tracking with enough flexibility that I can share some lists with my wife and kids.

Personal productivity software is new to me. But this app is pretty exceptional, especially when paired with the desktop version (included in subscription). I have learned to use nearly every feature, and it's all been very helpful. Also I really appreciate the ethical choices and high quality of the app. The free version is genuinely helpful and contains no ads. And the paid version is reasonably priced and you can easily cancel the subscription immediately and still receive the full year.

Easy to add tasks, include attachments. This has kept my ADD brain on schedule better than any other program. You can personalize or keep as it is. I appreciate how you can choose how you can see your tasks in different formats. You can choose to be reminded by location, or time. The timer can go on until you dismiss, as an option. So many great usable things for the person that wants to be organized.

I loved the widgets this app has! It has whatever you need for a checklist and habit tracker. But the pro version is a little bit pricy, if it had one-time pay, i would have gotten it, but good for overall quality. Edit: I've got it. Please add linking tasks with subtasks to habits. So we can benefit from checking a task in Notion, that will automatically check habits in TickTick 🙌

I love using it as a to-do list and to keep track of my bills! (Feb/2021) Update: It is now Dec. 31 2024 and I just uninstalled the app. Obviously I was very pleased with it until recently. I used this app for EVERYTHING. I had 6-7 lists and I used it daily. However I was trying to "organize" several lists and a message popped up that I'd never seen before: I had USED aa many tasks as they would allow and I would need to purchase more. I'm not going to play that game. I'm out like saurkraut. ✌

As others have noted, the app is now "asking" permission to associate you with your personal info on Google, but it won't accept Deny. The request always pops back up half a second later, ad infinitum, making the app effectively unusable. I had to be quick and try multiple times to even open up the Settings to try to make a report through the app, but, of course, the report won't send. I get a message saying "Something wrong. Please try again later." So, I'm complaining here instead.

Life changing! It was an easy choice to pay for TickTick. I've spent more on paper planners that I've used for a month and then it inevitably would sit unused the rest of the year. I've learned since receiving a late diagnosis of ADHD that I need to write everything down and I have to be able to trust my calendar! TickTick makes it so easy to add stuff and keep it all organized. I love that it automatically detects dates and times, so it's not like a whole lot of different fields to fill in.

May 2025 update: Suddenly, this app constantly shows a box telling me it can't connect with Google, asking me to update my password. Please help! So far, TickTick has worked very well for me. I transferred all my lists from Wunderlist and just had to narrow down the number of lists to 9. Everything is simple and clear. I wish there was a way to easily make all my lists alphabetical as I could in Wunderlist.

This app does everything you wish Google Calendar did and looks good doing it. My personal productivity is up in the 2 weeks I've been using it. I used the free version for 48 hours before deciding to upgrade and was instantly glad I did the next time I opened the app. For real, it's $36 a year. That's like 10 cents a day and you'll feel like you're robbing them. The people behind this app are constantly working on it too, so it's not some static vaporware that never gets updated. They EARN it.

Super helpful!!! but... the app is only as good as the user. it's perfect for implementing and sticking to a daily routine, tracking habits, and staying focused. but it doesn't really do much to help you come up with that routine. as a neurodivergent individual I believe this app would be incredibly helpful if I were to work with a counselor/mentor to work out a detailed and personalized daily routine, goals and a general organization system - then use this app to implement it

Update: I paid for the Premium version and it lasted for a year or so and then my account reverted to the unpaid and limited my features again. Easy to use, flexible, and even the free version offers enough features to be absolutely useful. I use it for work reminders instead of sticky notes, for my shopping list, for Christmas lists, and to keep track of which monthly bills have been paid among other things.

Update May 2020: still love it! I've tried a lot of apps to keep track of lists over many years, and this is by far the best I've found. I love the seamless integration across the 4 devices I use - a Windows PC, 2 android tablets, and a Samsung phone. It's very easy to add notes for tasks, to put them into folders, to create subfolders, to change due dates, to select a group of tasks and make batch changes, and on and on. I'm a tough critic for most apps, but this one is a winner!

I have been on the hunt for a great Task organization app for quite some time. Have tried several including Wonderlist, and all have fallen short of TickTick. Clean interface, multiple themes including a great dark mode. I only wish the premium mode was a one time App purchase rather than a subscription. Other than that, I highly recommend.

2nd edit: the developer said it does record completion time. Premium Only and I don't have that. Fail. Edit: I wish it recorded what time I marked an item... I use this as a medication reminder, alarms and all. Why? because the 2 others I used a lot which did almost everything I wanted, have major problems I can't deal with. Eg, the alarm with one was to wake the screen, but didn't most of the time. That made turning the alarm off impossible until I could wake the screen, which it blocked.

Finally an app that helps me be more effective without a paper list! Light, fast, adaptive...and that's just in the free version! update 5-15-19 this little app continues to help me get things done! Love the updates! So adaptable and customizable for all my to-do's! I don't know what I would do without it. Update 5/1/22: continues to improve! I still have the free version (I'm retired so it works for me) and am more than happy with capabilities and the ease of adapting to my needs.

As a Wunderlist refugee, I love this app. I tried using Microsoft To Do but it feels too plain and doesn't have natural language, which is REALLY important to me. Other apps like Todoist don't provide important features in the free version, but TickTick provides all the necessities for free and has a few extras in the premium version, as it should be. I also can't stop using the Pomo timers. LOVE IT! Don't know if I'll ever go back to To Do, even after they add onto it!

I needed a replacement for Wunderlist, which we relied on for task lists for our business & employees. With TickTick, I had to manually type in each task for each list because the Import feature only put them in my Inbox. There's no Home Screen - hitting the return arrow exits the app (not a biggie, just an adjustment). The real downer is having to pay to share the lists with more than one person. Collaboration was an important feature we needed. Using Microsoft's To Do for now.

The app is pretty good. I downloaded it about 3 weeks ago and tried out the premium version because I NEED a monthly calendar view of my to-do items. (I schedule up to 2 weeks out, so a 7-day view just doesn't cut it.) I was planning on continuing to use it periodically whenever I ended up having a busy month, but sometime in the last 3 weeks, the price jumped from about $2.79 a month to $3.99. That's too much just for a monthly calendar view and not needing the rest of the premium features.

Best I've found so far. Very easy to organize. I use it daily for all my bills, shopping and so forth. The widgets you can add to home screen look lovely and have all the info you want right there. For my bills, for example, you can set up an item to occur every month. So I'll input Verizon $75 on Jun 10. And after I check it off, it'll drop to bottom of list. Checking off other items will hence bring it back toward the top. But the reminder will always be for the day (Monday) or date (10th).

Works well to track regular and time-based tasks from your phone or the web. The free version has a generous set of features that rivals some paid services. Automatic syncing isn't always as fast as I'd like (between the web and the Android app) but that's a minor complaint. There is a bug with the current version that converts a weekday recurring task created from the phone app to a weekly task on the web. I've reported it and hope they'll release a fix soon.

Absolutely amazing. A simple design that allows you to organize your task into a list that you can format however you want, and see on your phone, computer, or Android Wear. The only reason I give it 4 stars instead of 5 is because the syncing between devices is a little bit inconvenient. Often, I would make changes to my tasklist on my phone, and they would not show up on computer until I went back into the app and manually refreshed it.

scary. it's a great program (haven't put enough into it to see if it has the gtasks sync issues) but I don't see a backup option. I've put information into apps then, years later, they change formats and all data is lost. This can never happen. I need to know that I can always access a given version of the program and local data will always work. todo and Microsoft onenote burned me. (Microsoft is extorting, buy office 365 and we'll allow you access to your prior data.)

THE BEST task management app I've ever used (and I've tried dozens). It's simple to use and has a nice user interface, but it's got the robust features under the hood and out of your way for when you need them, for example advanced recurring tasks, multiple notifications, habit tracking, calendar sync, location-based reminders, search, tagging, etc. I started with the free trial for a year or two. I've now been a happy premium user for over 4 years.

I love TickTick - I use it for everything. Very well thought through To Do task list, just amazing. Only thing I don't like is that they have raised the price, used to be 1.99 per month, but has gone up to 2.79 a month from what I can see. Longer I use it, more entangled I get with it - maybe the price will double again next year?! Hope not. Update Feb 2019; tried other task lists but none come close to TickTick so far and it just keeps getting better with update, sticking with it for now.

So far, TickTick has been great -- much more enjoyable than the previous task management app I had (Tasks). For starters, my notifications actually pop up and work more often than the other app. I also love the easy-to-use interface and the different layouts/calanders available (though some are premium). Just a better looking, more functional every day app for any task you can imagine! I have had some notifications not pop up for whatever reason. I'll be keeping an eye on that. Overall, like it!

Has been one of the best reminder app available for a long time. This most recent update messed up notifications on Android 10. They don't follow the system dark mode at all, instead you have to manually change it in the app or deal with mismatched colors. It looks ridiculous seeing a white notification background amidst other black backgrounds in dark mode. It also looks like the black is not a true black like other apps. If this isn't fixed soon, I'll be looking for a new reminder app.

Simple and intuitive. I don't even know for sure how long I've been using TickTick, but at the time I tried multiple free to-do list apps, and this one was by far my favorite. It allows my tasks to auto reset on a schedule of my choice. I get reminders for the day, but can also set reminders ahead of time (down to a specific time of day if preferred).

This is a very good app for keeping track of my busy day. I've actually replaced Google Calendar because TickTick can do so much more. The subscription is reasonable, especially considering what is included. If you purchase the subscription, uninstall and reinstall the app. Otherwise, it appears you will lose sync functionality across devices.

Great app. I have used the GTD method of organizing for nearly 20 years on many apps (going back to the Palm Pilot days if you can imagine) and this app is one of the best I have ever seen for that. I like how it combines a calendar with an organizer and it meets a need other apps haven't provided. Only improvement I wish to see would be that it could be easier to make calendar entries into the app directly. I have to got through a lot of steps to do that. While the app integrates outside calendars nicely, I would like this to replace my regular calendar.

Nothing better. Not sure how many years I have used this, 5+ years @least, I have tried everything out there, but nothing works as well. First, it's easy. Second, it's minimal, I don't spend more time setting up, organizing, etc. I spend my time doing what I need, not taking time in the app Third, it's every where I need to access it. Is just easy and fast and I particularly love the reminder feature. I used it free for awhile, but have happily had premium for years.

So far so good! I've been using the free version for a week now, and I'm loving it! I'm still learning to get the full potential for my needs, but it handles list and recurring events, with ending dates and times (detailed and Personalized), the way I was looking for. I love the Eisenhower Matrix, probably the payed version customizes better than the free one, but I love it, as that's something I learned in the book, and keep in mind through the day. I will switch to the payed full version soon.

The only app I'll ever use for to-do anything. Simple, customizable, great as a calendar, you can organize things to a point where it's almost insane if you choose, (imo) intuitive UI and shortcuts, enough options to suit pretty much anyone's needs. It's great without the monthly sub but I still have it because it's cheap ($2.99) and I use the app daily. The only things I would like added are more theme options and a way to "complete" recurring tasks, but that's a nitpick at this point lol.

This app is so great, it can really fit so many different types of peoples needs. Creating basic checklists is super easy. But if you need more advanced ways for organization they have it! You can create so many lists, tags, and smart lists. They all have there functionality and a super easy set up. Not to mention the habit checker, while you can only have 3 with the free version it is a nice addition.

This is the only todo app that has ever worked for me. They got it right: due dates and reminders are completely separate. You can map out todos on your calendar in terms of when they're due, but still set complex reminders to pace you as you approach that date. Everything I've wished it could do, it just does in a natural way. With other todo apps, I'm always left wondering why it can't handle the natural use case, and it just ends up getting in the way. I feel less stressed than ever.

Almost perfect!!! This app is my every-day checklist, my cleaning task list, my grocery list, everything!!! The only reason I can't give it 5 stars is the weird, passive-aggressive pop up I get whenever the app decides it's "not being useful" and offers to turn off reminders. I don't always finish my to do list every single day, so these weird "so sorry to bother you!" messages genuinely get on my nerves. I emailed the developer about three months ago to ask if I could turn it off, no response.

Very good reminder app. Alarms are very persistent; I wish I could select individual sounds for each alarm though. I also wish I could edit a snooze after I set it. And I wish I could postpone an upcoming alarm for one instance without postponing all future alarms. Update (4/8/2021): Thank you for adding a location setting for both arriving and leaving a location. Super helpful. Now, if you could get your app to sync with Google Assistant so I can create task while I'm driving.

Tick Tick is great. My daughter recommended it to me. She likes the natural language option. I love the option to create habits out of recurring to do tasks, that's important for me, as I'm using Tick Tick in part to try to put my life back together after my husband's death. It's very helpful to have a checklist and form habits that are little in the moment but cumulatively make a big difference. Still trying to figure out how to not have a date for a list item though (for book/grocery lists).

Had some weird hiccups and things to first figure it out, but got it going smoothly. Love the option to share todo lists, and the integration with other calendars. Stand alone web version also good, widget isn't great, but all around a very solid app. No intrusive ads, and a robust free version that is still worth using it you don't want to pay the monthly fee. Highly recommend trying it out.

So, I really like this app! Probably one of the best planner apps out there, but I ended up uninstalling it because it kept asking to pair with my Google account, and the pop-up was rather aggressive. The cancel button didn't work and it would pop up after every single action. Even after going to the settings to try to sync it with my account, I didn't see a way to sync it! I do really love this app, it's great! But that pop up just ruined it

2016: Best "To Do" list that I have ever found. I know my tasks for today, if i want to get a jump on tomorrow or what I have missed in the past As of 2/25/21...App went nuts, relentlessly asking for all permissions to my phone in a pop-up that wouldn't stop. Uninstalled and reinstalled only to find everything from the last year was gone-- all my reminders--everything. Been asking for help through app and a week later I am still no closer to restoration. Paper and pen are more reliable. Be wary.

this was one of the 1st apps I tried when I started looking for a productivity, task, ect app but still tried a few other ones & now after a cpl years im right back where I started! There is a 7-Day Premium Trial to see if itll work for you. The only thing that would make it easier to choose is if there was a 14-30 day trial that way ppl can use it for at least a cpl weeks so they can see if it works for them during multiple situations & not just a week.. my weeks/even days fluctuate ALOT!

Free version is an excellent tool, and it's not so limited that it feels like you have no choice but to pay for it to get something useful. Works wonders, and has massively helped me organize, remember/plan events, and manage my time and focus. It's quick, lightweight, with an interface that makes sense. Fantastic for day-to-day use.

Missing some basic features, but overall good. Biggest flaw is lack of sorting or management of checked off items. When you mark eggs off your shopping list, you have to hunt through the checked items to find out again later, when an alphabetized list would make it so efficient. Our you just add eggs again to the point you eventually have eggs in the completed list 7 times. That problem alone is serious enough for me to drop 2 stars off. Fix that and I'd rate it a 5.

a really well rounded, useful app, it's really flexible & customisable. there's just a few things that hold it back, like the list of tasks not being shown in chronological order even though I've set times for them all, not being able to edit a task once it's created, the calendar has multiple view options but none of them are particularly easy/good to look at. it works better on desktop than mobile and I wish it was a desktop app rather than Web browser. few small tweaks would make it perfect

Like it. Notifications & popups are great for constant reminders! They are cute & look nice as well! Helps keep me focused on what I need to be. Only thing that I would reccomend for others who install, would be: Its tempting to make many reminders because they're so great, but, if you make more than a few at a time, they pop up constantly & become an annoyance, & easily ignored. Focus on just a few reminders, then, when they become HABITS, take them off and put up new goals.🤠

FINALLY a app that understands the difference between a task, habit, and a event. Love TickTick so far, using for a week or so, feels fluid and easy to use like Wunderlist was, but much better and free of the bugs. It looks like habits are not working on the web app version, but that is ok as I mainly use it on my phone. Great product! Will likely upgrade and be happy to pay for the premium version.

I'm using the free version of TickTick, and for what I'm paying, it deserves five stars. I can make detailed to do lists with multiple actions under one subheading and can categorize my lists to make it easier to see what I'm doing (and what I could be doing if I find myself bored). You can schedule your tasks to repeat with a lot of custom settings. I appreciate the functionality of this app!

Update 3/8/21: They won't respond to my inquiry. Update 3/3/21: Won't let me change payment method. While trying to do so, I finally got to a link that would allow me to "manage or cancel my premium membership," but the link doesn't work. Evidently, then, I'm not able to cancel if I want to either. I also went to the Help menu, but there's nothing there to help with this either. Works very well and as expected so far. 7 months later: It's still working great.

They double billed me for my premium subscription and wont respond to any refund requests. I have been a premium subscriber for over a year, and changed my payment method from Paypal to Google Play subscription, but ended up being billed twice at my renewal (once through each method). I've reached out to their support email address (4 times), and haven't even gotten a response. This app is a good app, but you cannot trust them with your financial information or expect them to respond to you if you have a problem.

Great app! Great price, actually way underpriced for what you get. I was able to delete at least 2 other apps and use this as my go-to for everything. I am a "planner" and this gives you every way to plan you could want. Great design, user friendly, highly recommend. Bonus is the plan you day feature which you won't find on a lot of other apps. I tried several before coming across this one, & so glad I did! Also, the all device sync (including PC), & widgets are amazing!!

Extremely powerful and customizable app. Great widgets. It takes some time to poke around all of the available settings, but you can set up a widget to do just about anything you can imagine. View your tasks and lists the way you want to. I like the sync between the mobile app and desktop browser experience. Also easy to back up, although I do wish there was an automatic backup/storage sync feature.

EDIT: Trying out the app once again and find it more useful than before. Actually the pricing is cheaper than other similar apps with less features. I've requested a one day trial to see if the additional features are needed for my particular situation. I hope they approve it. I'm pretty sure I will go for yearly subscription.

Really like the app. Its great for keeping various tasks organized. I use it to track the status of many dozen simultaneous maintenance tasks at multiple locations. It's great for this and especially because lists can be shared amongst multiple users and tasks can be assigned. Rating lost a star because changing from monthly to annual subscription has been a nightmare and I'm afraid I may lose all my data.

Overall, the app is fine and works for me. However with the latest version, they changed the order of the "Snooze" and "Complete" buttons in the notification. For anyone like myself who has used the app for years, this results in many times accidentally clicking the wrong one. This change adds no value at all but will frustrate any users who have previously used these notification action buttons!

This is a very good reminder app. Reminders entered on mobile or web site sync perfectly. The only thing missing is a Due-like (Apple only) ability to issue annoying notifications. With TickTick, you get one notification. If you're not near your phone, you won't know you've missed a task until next time you pick up your phone. It'd be great if TickTick would issue a *new* notification every X minutes (user-controlled) until you acknowledge, so that you never miss a notification.

I was so pleased with the new Habit tracker as well as using a true Dark Mode. However, I don't agree with the Premium policy as I prefer to make a bigger one-time payment (such as when I bought Final Cut Pro X with Apple) instead of a small recurring subscription that makes me want to go back to Todoist. It's a damn shame as I liked TickTick so much I'm willing to pay for Premium. Unfortunately, the subscription policy is an easy deal breaker.

I have ADHD and this app really helps me to organize and structure tasks i regularly forget. I haven't run into a situation where i would need to upgrade to the premium service. You can schedule things to happen every set amount of time (i have one to remind me to clean my pet's cage every 2 weeks). You can make different categories, so I have work, school, groceries, home. You can also schedule something for a specific time or date! Very useful when i think of something i need to do later.

We have been using TickTick for a few years for home and personal management. Love that i can customize in so many different ways when things can repeat. A feature i would love to have is being able to share a folder with one or more other people. Everyone who is sharing the folder could then add items or click that it is done without seeing everything else in my account. I like the features that are in the laptop version and not in the phone app so I mostly use that version.

I use this for all my to-do lists, shopping lists, and all kinds of other lists and notes. I originally started using it when GTasks went out of support because it has a similar UI. A few things like list sections are not that easy to use but mostly it's quite intuitive, syncs across all my devices, and has all the scheduling, alerts, and priority setting that I need. 9/2024 just updating to say I still use this app all the time.

Exactly what I needed! using the free version, which has its restrictions, but retains plenty of functionality and is very useful and helpful. Able to share/collaborate which is endlessly great for grocery shopping and the like. Clean and easy to use UI. Super highly recommended to anyone wanting to organize their lives with checklists.

I downloaded this app and I didn't expect much but its honestly amazing! I love the simple layout and everything about it. I love that you can add or delete tools like for me, I wanted to have a habit tracker and I could simply enable that. I can clearly see why it is editors choice! Recommend it to everyone! ❤ Edit: You can only do 5 habits with subscription sadly.

Only good Calendar App. Has to-do lists integrated with its calendar. The idea that this is a revolutionary concept tells you how bad every other one is. Despite what it claims, it is not a notetaking app. This is entirely forgivable. Pay the subscription. I, like everybody else, am a cheapskate, but I cannot stress enough how much better the paid version is. The free version of this app is lobotomized. If you are using that version of this app long-term, you are lobotomized. Also, it's only 2$.

I use this app all the time! I have lists that I return to, such as my grocery list, where I can easily add back previously crossed-out items or add new items. I also have work lists, weekend lists, wish lists, etc. It's a flexible app that allows you to use it in the best way for your needs.

Took a little getting used to. Once I did, I found it to be the best app I've ever tried for tracking To Do items. The web client is great on the work PC. Powerful and complete in it's capabilities! Within a few days, I knew I was gonna use Tick Tick ALOT, so I cancelled my monthly subscription and changed it to annual. I'm a fan. 5 stars.

My review might be a little skewed because I'm looking for something specific. I want an app to create an hourly task today, that doesn't require me to enter in a reminder for every hour in the day. I don't really need a task manager because I have 3 built into other apps, and I don't need a place to keep notes to be reminded about because I also have several apps that do that. Really I just want a flexible natural language reminder app.

I have tried multiple kinds of planner apps and this is the best of them all. It is the only one I found that lets me make recurring to-dos, have everything in a calendar view and give each task a duration (paid features), track habits, have focus sessions, subtasks, reminders, get summaries about productivity, and sync with all my devices (MacBook, iPad, and android phone). The free version is good but I highly recommend the paid version. I am more motivated and productive because of this app.

I'm sold! I've tried so many task lists, habit trackers, calendars, pomodoro apps, pocket link storing and this is the ONE! It does all of it in a single app. It allows for sharing task lists. It has folders, tags, and smart lists. It has a my day interface to help start and end the day. It integrates with Google calendars. Has a gmail and chrome extension. I don't use it for notes, but you can and it allows for the same tagging, formatting, and folder features. I love this app!

With someone who has ADHD, I would recommend this to-do, habit, and calendar list for anyone who prefers very simple instructions on how to set up stuff that you need. A lot of the tasks are movable, have a variety of options for repeatability, and can be accessed through desktop and mobile (making it easier for productivity and remembering). My only qualms would be that features are in a paywall, but the base purchase is very ADHD accessible regardless.

Confusion regarding 'sharing' your schedule with other team members or subscibers. Seriously, would it kill them to put an 'added', 'enter', or some kind of 'task completed' button... it would save a lot of confusion. Sending an invite to fellow workers results in a vague link that seems disconnected with the original intention. No 'Connect with Users' or anything other than a link. For something that's supposed to be time management related, I've wasted hours trying to figure this out.

I've been using TickTick for probably 4 years, and it's the best to-do list app out there. It's simple and straightforward, and I love that I can have it on all of my devices and computer, and keep one to do list wherever I go. I would love to give it 5 stars, but over the last few months it's been very glitchy with syncing between my various devices.

Love it. Absolutely love it. EDIT: 4 years later, and it is still my favorite. I randomly try out others, and I'm just happy with this. Location based reminders are awesome. I don't want to be reminded to take out the trash while I'm out. I want the reminder as soon as I get home. The little things matter so much. Edit: Still using it. I've tried others just to compare. They just don't compare. I dropped over 100 tasks into TickTick in 30 secs using Excel. This is my bujo.

Could you please make it so that when the habit tracker pops up and asks me if I completed my habit for the day I can say no? All I can do is swipe to say "completed". If I want to say I didn't do it, I have to open the app and manually enter in that I did not complete it. I'm trying to track addiction from fast food but the app doesn't allow you to set goals properly. If I say I want to do something for 30 days in a row it should reset the counter every time I relapse but it doesn't.

I paid for the annual membership after trying the free version for a few days because I really wanted it to work, but after trying to use premium for a few days, I realized that this program was packed with features but has an absolutely unintuitive interface. I've tried using it here and there, but it's just frustrating and anxiety inducing. I advise using and loving the free version for at least a month before committing to membership.

Love this app!! It's exactly what I've spent years looking for! Einstein matrix, various displays and organizational methods for lists and task, calendars (you can import from personal, work, etc). Plus the habit tracker with an Pondo timer!!! The widgets are so fun, it makes me want to use it, thus I'm utilizing my time better, so it really is working.

Been using this app for years. I used to give it 5 stars but over the past year it has gotten worse and worse. New features, yes, and the Android app works well enough, but I prefer typing on a real keyboard so I use the Windows app and web based version. Those have a lot of interface/interaction bugs, which support doesn't seem to care about. The web app has a ridiculous lag when typing and the Windows app randomly moves the cursor on you. I would change my rating if they would finally fix.

Transitioned over to Tick Tick from Wunderlist over a year ago and loved it. However in the last few days a new pop up has appeared asking for permission to sync with my Google account. When I press deny it pops up again less than a second later and continues indefinitely. On the rare occasion I was able to access this app I tried to get over to settings and log in a complaint, only to be met with "something is wrong please try later" when I hit submit. This is unacceptable, please fix this!

3 stars only because I can't see ALL my tasks in the first tab, and can only view by "folder" (inbox, tags, family, bills...). There is a "Today" view but that doesn't help since you can miss the future tasks as you're not prepared for them. It's bothersome because I will have to look at Eisenhower view to see all the tasks by order of importance only. Free easy to add, view, and clear tasks as well as add notes or reminders. It integrates into Google or other calendars too.

I am only using the free version but I love this app already. I really enjoy that it is seamless across all my android devices and on my computer. While it is a bit easier to create lists on my computer, it is still not too difficult on my devices. This has been really helpful to keep my chores and responsibilities in order and getting done on time. I have recommended it to many people already! I haven't been late and forgetting things since I started.

Overall a good app. Synchronization is terrible, constantly losing information when using multiple devices. One can make changes on a computer and not see changes on the phone for minutes. If you close the computer app immediately you may never see those changes. Really it's 2 star, but the app is great compared to other trash on the market.

Paid version is probably great. I used this app years ago before it became cluttered with features I didn't use. Since my current reminder app became outdated I decided to give this one another shot. It looks like all the kinks have been worked out and it could be a great app, but I think some of the features you have to pay for and the ones that are free should be switched. For example, the monthly calendar is a paid option but weekly view is free. Also, you must pay for repeat reminders.

It works as a reminder system somewhat. Difficult to find the repeat buttons. Difficult to find reminders. I think I would be able to just hit the three dots and they would be listed there. Also difficult to reschedule. So, I feel like I need to find a YouTube video to explain how it works. I put in some things that are reminders to get them done eventually. I find it defaults to the day you entered them so I have a bunch of items that are overdue. It is NOT intuitive to use.

SO ANNOYING!! At first I liked the feature where I could change the repeat type to date of completion, but then it started having a random chime that went off at 9am, 12noon, and 6pm for NO REASON! My notifications were silenced, and they didn't even show up in the notification history and it was driving me CRAZY! I had to download another app to tell me what was making the sound. And THEN, I thought I switched it off in the app settings and it STILL DID IT. So I'm done, deleted from my phone.

Overall this is a great product. I find it intuitive, easy to use, productive, and it keeps me on track. However, it has one annoying "feature". There's a setting to enable/disable the adjustment of reminder times when a change in time zone is detected. I have it disabled. I live near the border between two states in different time zones and travel be them regularly. Instead of respecting the setting, it constantly asks me if I want to enable it. NO, I DON'T! AND STOP ASKING! Fix this - 5 stars.

Digging this app! I like the habit tracking and countdown features. Those are what set this app apart from competitors like Todoist. The only feature that I wish was there but isn't is automatic backups. Right now, as far as I know, you have to manually backup and save the file somewhere. Still a great app though!

This app is amazing,I've been using it since February and it's the only to do app that I liked. it helped me finish my tasks much faster and kept me consistent. I do wish that we could edit the color of little dots that would be on the bottom of the dates when you have something planned on that date. It would really help me recognize which date has the more important task and the least important ones for my rest days!!

Used to pay for it but trying the fre version now due to student economy. Both versions work well for the day to day management but the problem of integrating it with other apps like Google calender is making me concider moving away from this app to other alternatives. All in a all, a good app though.

Hello, the ticktick app is overall quite decent, however there are some annoying bugs, for example how a subtask under a task will sometimes just 'disappear' and be replaced with an exact copy of another subtask. Also, I would appreciate the ability to search through deleted tasks. Thank you.

I tried TickTick based on recommendations for ADHD-friendly task management, expecting a simple app to organize lists and prioritize tasks. Unfortunately, it’s been overwhelming with too many features like Pomodoro timers and habit tracking that clutter the interface. After months, I have dozens of unmanageable tasks and still struggle to find what I need. While it may work for those wanting customization, it's not ideal if you’re looking for something basic and easy to use.

This is the best list app out there. I use it to make lists of everything: daily routines, work tasks, shopping lists, hobby ideas, etc. I love being able to set up schedules and reminders that are easy to snooze or reschedule. The priority flags are very helpful for letting me know what needs to be done and what can wait. I also love the built in pomodoro timer and the ability to track habits! This is one of the very few apps where I pay for premium, and it's absolutely worth it.

The best app for organising your life. Put reminders of what you need to do so you will not forget. Schedule recurrent takes like paying bills or adding meter readings. Birthday, anniversary with multiple reminders (different times/days) so you will remember to prepare for them. Great search, this way you can write down everything you want to keep and find it easily, like how much pressure to put in your car tyres, pants size and anything else you can think of. This is just the free part of it.

The app itself works great, smooth, effective, and completely ad-free but the free version is limited, and the paid version is subscription-only. It’s frustrating how some tech companies push recurring payments for something you could just do with a simple note-taking app or even pen and paper. I’d happily pay a one-time fee, even a high one, but I refuse to subscribe for something this basic.

Literally everything you need for productivity. I've been using it for years, I can't live without it. Simply the best productivity app out there (believe me, i've tried most of them). Programmable filters, customizable icons, removable buttons in the UI, this app has it all. Very useful sync options (import calendars, export tasks etc.) too. Ideal for power users like me, and the app is still very easy to understand. Also works great on PC (desktop / web app). Plus the free version is amazing!

Absolutely love this app. I have ADHD and have tried so many different apps to stay organised. With TickTick, everything is in one place and has pomos built in too. Really useful. My only criticism would be that it is a shame there is no basic calendar feature on the free version. I would have liked to share calendars with my husband. The annual fee is reasonably priced but we don't both want to pay for it.

Working with TickTick for 2 weeks now, and after many other similar apps, TickTick is finally the app that works for me. Wish I had found it earlier. Every app needs some getting used to; however, TickTick is really simple but also powerful—perfect for my tasks and also notes. On top of everything, it's also not ridiculously expensive like other apps. It works fluently for me, and that's the most important!

Great checklist app! I only use the free version and it serves its purpose really well with only some bonuses on the paid version I'd be interested in. Great for jotting things down to remember, really easy task formatting such as sections. Great for projects with multiple steps/subtasks. Connecting up with my Google calendars has been useful as well. The new look with backgrounds etc is refreshing too. Please keep the free version as awesome as it is!

The best I've tried. Easy to use while still being easy customizable. I like: - pomodoro and Eisenhower features - organizing the tasks with a priority flag or label - synchronizing the calendar with others - using it on any device (phone, tablet, laptop) - the general design that you can easily customize - different calendar views - shortcuts (e.g. writing 10:00 -> the task begins at 10) What would be nice: - having the possibility to write notes using a pen on the tablet (like onGoodNotes)

I have been using this app for almost 1 year. In my opinion, this is the best app to track our daily activities and maintain our discipline daily. This app has helped me a lot for planning professional and personal activities in the last year. For example, I am using this app for tracking my daily exercise, diet, etc...

Very interesting app, with useful features and far more better than many I've used, but it lacks an important feature, the ability to edit the tasks imported from external calendars. You can only mark as done the local tasks. the external are only listed. Suggestion: Tasks from external calendars could be copied to Tick Tick Data base, and edited like the other tasks created locally. Some other task management tools have this feature implemented and it makes difference. Please consider.

I prefer ticktick over Todoist due to its features all in one app. Ticktick has all the features I needed in one app! E.g. Pomodoro/Stopwatch, Habits, Eisenhower Matrix, and Calendar (premium needed to sync calendar tho). It also has statistics of focused time. It also has categories, tags, and priority to manage your tasks further. It also can use the word recognition i.e. writing "tomorrow" will make the task due tomorrow. Also have sync with desktop and web. Thanks devs. Keep it up!

Earlier this app was working perfectly when I installed it and used it for a few weeks. Reminders were coming on time and everything was smooth. But after some time, I completely stopped receiving reminder notifications. Because of this, I missed important work. The app fails at its core purpose — reminding the user. I have checked notification settings, battery optimization, and permissions, but still the issue continues. If an app cannot reliably send reminders, it’s not useful. Not a great ap

amazing app i love it so much. has so many features and doesn't force you to pay for basic features like some other apps do. keep everything very organised. one recommendation though would be to add deadlines. dates are already there but sometimes the due date could be later but we may have the time to do it a day before that. having a seperate deadline would be very handy and useful. pls do take this into consideration. other than that, splendid app!!

Is it possible to display the color of the task even in the widget (compact widget)? As of now it is displayed only when opening the app. In addition: is it possible to choose which list to display in the pop up list-chooser window? When I click on the current list in the widget, I get to choose among a lot of different lists like "today" "tomorrow" etc. I want just my 2 lists "work" and "personal", not a huge list of 7 different entries. Thanks

what an amazing app! I love how I can organize tasks by priority and date and flag them appropriately. I also utilized this app as a trip planner and it has scratched the organization itch in me! It is so amazing to have flights tagged in via email link, plus checklist for sites to see. Do not hesitate to download this app! Everything you need is free, and whatever is not is for sure worth the paid subscription!

Great app! The animations after you log a habit is just top notch. Kindly add Journaling and Let us make other calenders as main, decide when the day starts for us, save a month/year/day as important or fav or create a folder with those important logs. like I got to save the ramadhan logs and revisit quickly the next year.

Remains the better task mgt app. can't give 5 stars because the timeline view is half-baked. Tasks in lists only display in the browser, not on this phone app nor in the desktop app. I was really excited about that view so it's disappointing. Also still wishing it had a time tracker for invoicing (pomodoro sux for that).

ADHD sufferer here. I like the UI and the easy customizable organization for your priorities (I personally hate the Eisenhower method, so it's nice that you can hide it). The ability to pick a widget to make it instantly visible when you open your phone is SUCH a great feature. I'll be testing it out more, though I have notes so far. And unfortunately, this is yet another planner app that locks basic functions behind a HEFTY paywall. $50 a YEAR to set a task time duration is wild.

I've never used such a comprehensive, straightforward task management app. It keeps me motivated to finish my graduate work with things like pomodoro/time tracking. I can just dump things in there, sort it, and Get Things Done (GTD is seamless in this app!). Ive tried finding both digital and analog replacements and nothing competes.

I'm sure it's still a great to-do app. it was the best I found after trying many. but I want a to do app I don't have to sign in to. Would rather send you money than sign in. So it's gone and I'm looking for something else again. Personally, too, I just want a to-do app. not all the add-ons.

I do really like this app and I use it since years, I do not have enough money else I would have upgraded my account. One request it would be better to keep track of old finished. Tasks as memory that can be seen by users something that could be even nostalgic after period of time.

Great app with lots of features, but maybe not great for the ADHD brain? I really like all of the features & customization options, but I need SIMPLE. BASIC. ALMOST BORING. So many features/customization options that it's too overwhelming to be helpful or useful to me. Not very intuitive UI in my opinion. Unfortunately I paid for the premium version before really trying it out to see if it would work for me. This app is great for people who can effectively micro-organize; my brain can't. :/

using it for 3 years now, best to do app ever, has all the features+ habit counter. what I like most is it has everything you need from a productiy hack, does reminders perfectly, has habit tracker,.notes tracker, ensinhowwr metrics. and it's cheap. love it

i will say it is amongst the best productivity apps that I used or use daily. I am a student and i really like its pomo feature I use it daily so overall a great app yup many things are behind the pay wall but the free version can also be enough for most people out there .
Outstanding App. Appointments, alarms, notifications, notes, flexibility...this has been my go to app for years. None better in my opinion. I've used it everyday for years! Great. Great. Great.

been using it for 3 months. i prefer it heavily over alternatives like todoist and Google Tasks (yikes), which feel limited in comparison. it's an all in one app. I use it for my tasks, my calendar, my habits and pomo timers. a few features are locked behind premium, like timelines, calendar views and quite a few good widgets... but I'll forgive it just because the app is that good.

This app is exactly what I was looking for - a clean, minimal UI that shows you the tasks you're supposed to do _today_. It also helps delay or postpone tasks that you couldn't do, to tomorrow or some other days. Also, using it on the phone and the desktop at the same time works flawlessly, and the free version is extremely clean and highly functional with no ads. It's an absolute must have!

TickTick has been essential for helping me keep track of all the little things I need to do all the time. The thing that made me get this five stars though was opening my phone and seeing a notification from the app that my premium subscription was going to renew. In a world of scummy apps that prey on people who forget to cancel subscriptions, seeing that TickTick does the opposite was worth five stars.

This has really helped me get organised, I love all the tools and highly recommend. It's also priced right, compared to competitors. my only negative, is that it takes time to learn and get used to, but worth it. Start small/basic explore as you go! would be my tip.

My favourite tool for time management. I drop all upcoming requests and task in the inbox and use Eisenhower's Matrix a lot for prioritisation. Also I love that I can use it on mobile and desktop. Small feature request: desktop app on Mac misses switching to dark mode after sunset (together with system settings). Also switching from monthly to yearly subscription is impossible when you have an active monthly subscription.

Best app on the market. I have used many to do list apps before, but this one is the most simple and useful. This has plenty of options to set personalised reminders. This app has everything I needed and more. (edit) The BEST app in the market and it only gets better. Logical updates. All-in-one solution.

One of the best task-sharing apps out there! My premium subscription is definitely worth it. I love how there are so many features in the user's disposal that could be shared to other users as well. Me and my friends have never been more efficient. I wish other apps could do the same non-capitalist practice TickTick does.

Possibly the best App on the Play Store. Developers have taken a bit of all other Productivity apps and combined the features into a top notch app. You can do pretty much anything you need for to do list, notes, attachments, reminders etc especially on the reasonably priced pro plan. They update it regularly and it works very reliably. It's helping me a lot to manage a really big workload. Worth every penny of the Subscription

TickTick is a great app! I don't think any other similar app exists, I've been using it for about a year and have you said every day from writing my goals to setting up daily tasks. I'm shocked how much you get in the free version (more than most other apps give you for a subscription).

Works great. Cross-platform works great. Web browser and app are intuitive. Sharing lists is easy. I have had trouble sharing calenders but it might just be something I'm doing wrong. But this app is magnitudes better than ToDo or any other app I have found. Convinced me to buy premium almost immediately.

It's an AMAZING app. Really changed how I manage my tasks & goals. It's made my life so much easier! I love how it's customisable, unique, truly understands its clients and offers a lot even on a free plan. It's really beneficial to have SO MANY features all organised in ONE app. I'd recommend this to anyone who wants to live their life a little more efficiently. Thank you for creating this app!

still figuring out how to best optimize the tick tick experience, but so far the day to day management of my life tasks has felt really great. I appreciate being able to toggle between different forms of organization, such as check lists, calendar views, and even the Eisenhower Matrix for when my brain needs a different way to think through my priorities. It's great! I just wish I would have found this app years ago.

It somehow co-op'd the "tap the clock in the notification swipe-down" to open... the next due item? I have no idea how that makes sense. A quick scroll through the app settings and nothing obvious about this arises. No hilights to call out changes to default phone settings. I am genuinely lost.

this is the best app i have ever used so far!!! used notion, click up, todoist and few other native apps but this one its solid. And iam planning to buy the year plan soon next month. but still I feel there are few bugs. For some reason I find Todoist is very smooth and friendly compared to tick tick. Also the chrome extension just opens up my task list it would be more useful if I can add any link as a task directly like how Todoist does. It auto detects that Iam in a web page and shows options

Used to love it. Now it's not opening to the calendar page, I gotta mess with it to get to my entries. I guess I can get used to pulling down to see the calendar that's not much different. Contemplating checking out the others. Love it. It works great for me. I hope they don't try to "improve" It much and try to add a bunch of new features. That usually doesn't work well.

I spent so long looking for an app that does all the things I need, integrates with most of my work and personal calendars, and has a nice looking interface (i.e. doesn't look like a live journal skin from 2005). This one has all of it and works how it should. As someone with ADHD this is all really important.

Incredibly customizable. I can make this work for pretty much any organizational system I want, that works with my peculiar ADHD brain. I don't even need the paid version with Google calendar, but I kind of want to subscribe anyway just to support the devs and get the built in calendar support. The only things I find myself missing are tracking support for weekly habits (it only includes daily habits) and custom notification sounds for different tasks.

Add feature that can connect reminder with phone app. For example "call John today at 1 pm." Then when it's time to call, it will dial the person (pulls the contact info in the phone). Any.do has this and I'm contemplating between your app and any.do. Then I'll switch.

barley acceptable. you can set a default category, not there is no quick change option, you put shopping list on the calendar in a specific date that the list was made. it's the dumbest thing ever, the app is so close to awesome. their UI sucks

A life-saver for a person like me who keeps on forgeting important tasks. I've tried all other reminder apps, trust me tick tick is the best. It never misses a reminder, others often do. Some Issues: 1. Reminder disappears after some time. 2. The contents of the reminder is not visible until I click on it and pop-up appears. Update: Issue 2 resolved. But not 1.

I just retested TickTick and am revising my old previous rating from 2 to a 5 stars. All connection and sync issues have been fixed and they've added a timeline view which I find very useful even if only just for planning. The views all work and they give what I need and not what I don't need... and I can categorize tasks by list and view by folder. This app kicks butt!

So many aspects that I would even give it 7-stars but my outstanding is the synchrony between 3 devices yet it is the free (basic) version. Thank you so much developers. I really needed this. (Will be considering premium somewhere in future when finances ease up) Thanks once again.

Good app! I would love it if you could also add more customization options. For example to add a setting that allows us to choose how many lines of details will be visible for a task, or a setting that allows us to customize the font color and size

Daily habit widget is too big and can't be resized. It takes up 3 rows with way too much padding above access below the actual widget square. Takes up far too much real estate so it has to live on my 3rd screen, making it difficult to access. Also can't seem to put anything on my lock screen so I can easily capture habits being completed.

The only to-do app I've found that does almost everything I need, even on the free plan. The premium plan offers the last few features I want too, and is cheaper than other premium plans I've seen. Would absolutely recommend if you're looking for an app with a few more features and customisability than other to-do apps. I've heard the devs are receptive to feedback too, which I appreciate. 10/10, solid app.
trust me when I say this, I really really loved the app.. it's easy to use and has all the essential features one needs. Just a quick feedback that adding a diary feature would make it even better!

Worked without issue and has many great features! I have been using the app for 6 months now and so far found it the best for tracking and prioritizing tasks. I also was surprised to find myself using timers a lot. Many things are customizable. UI has been intuitive and easy to use. it does everything I need it to do very well. Premium subscription is beneficial for students.

Came back to review this app again. To say this app has been an absolute game changer and essential to me is an understatement. After trying many todo apps this is the one that is stuck, and I have been happily paying for premium for probably 5 years now. Not only is it the most complete todo app, it keeps getting better with actually useful improvements every few months and devs that actually listen. Stop searching for your to do list app and pick this one - you won't regret it.

10/10 app, insanely good. the to do list format matches exactly what I wanted, links perfectly to the calendar. But one minor thing is that could you guys please allow us to make tasks reoccurring on the calendar? As in, if I have a task set for November 18th for instance, can it happen every year on November 18th? great app though, thank you guys so much! I love it!

Widgets have been broken for a week now. My to-do list just says, "No Title" when it was working just fine before. The habits also don't load. I rely greatly on these widgets to be reminded of the things I need to do, so I hope they get fixed soon.
I love this app. It is easy and simple to use while being very effective and well-organised. It improves my habits. In my mind, it became an urge to click the goals or habits I set.
Amazing. Clean interface, pomo timer, tag sistem, overall stat. there is probably a bug, I think I got some sounds from app when events on task come up. but all notifications for app are off

I got to a point where I genuinely struggled to keep track of tasks and routines, and eventually got to know about TickTick through a YouTube review. This app has been such a great help in managing time, visualizing tasks and routines, and setting reminders. So many helpful features to help you stay organized. I love it.

TickTick is just super exceptional. After getting frustrated with Todoist's limited feature set and limited controls (can't even change completion date, or have sensible overdue task behavior), boy is this a breath of fresh air. Not only does it address all of my frustrations, it's so so much more stuff I didn't know I needed. Proper note type, reminders, an awesome calendar view, habits (omg I love these), and so many powerful customization options to make it work exactly as I want.

Changing rating from 3* to 4*. Developers are implementing changes requested and required by there users but very slowly, a vast majority of feature requests still go unattended. Still improper documentation, no proper changelog, no road map, nothing. Most of important things are missing.

this app is just simply perfect, especially with the Eisenhower matrix, that actually helps to prioritise in which task to do first. would recommend for students as this has beatiful themes, along with tasks, habits and pomodoro timers. it also works perfectly fine for an outdated device. I would love 6 timers to add, instead of just 3, and more picture based themes, that are ACTUALLY free.....but overall it is good!!!!!!

When used in combination with the desktop app, this is the best planner / Todo / calendar / habit tracker I've ever used! I use the inbox widget on my home screen to log new tasks (thoughts) before I forget them, then I can sort them later. The Eisenhower matrix helps me to prioritise important tasks. I've also set up tags and categories to break up Work, Life, Household chores and Shopping lists. Then I drag and drop each task into a timeslot in my calendar. Best ADHD management app!

ADHDers, this is the one you want for you (and your fam), hyperfocus to set it up properly with your calendar and habits and recurring tasks. Use Reddit to help. This app is flexible enough for your dopamine-based list organisation to change over time.

Excellent app for work scheduling and daily task management! I have been using Tick Tack To-Do List for managing my daily work schedule, and it has been a great experience. The app is simple, clean, and very easy to use. Creating tasks, setting reminders, and tracking daily work has become much more organised and stress-free. It helps me plan my day efficiently and ensures that no important work is missed. The interface is smooth, lightweight, and perfect for professionals who need a reliable to

Best to do list out there and I've tried every single major ones so yeah, go tick tick. It is the best of breed, non intrusive, massive amount of options to manage your tasks given AND it's free. Update Nov 2025: Wish they had a one off lifetime payment for a subset of the premium functions. I am a light user and paying hundreds yearly for a advanced notepad is not feasible.
A great and simple app that does what it needs to do. 4-stars since it cannot be self hosted, but it was never advertised as such, so consider that lightly. Very reasonable subscription cost as well.

I've been using Google notes, and I'm alarmed at their invasion of my privacy. so I decided to look on Reddit and see what people consider to be the best app for note-taking. I'm trying this one out and I have until the 12th. update 2/24: I'm getting lost in this app. I can't find tasks. I can't find the calendar. I'm sorry I bought it.

It was the only habit app that eliminates the need for any other app because it also has pomodoro, calendar, notes, etc. Now I have one single centralized app that also has apps on Windows and every other operating system which is great for having your stuff on every device seamlessly. The 4 stars are because the Wear OS app is simply bad.

I absolutely love TickTick! We use it in our household under one shared account to keep track of everything that needs to be done, and in our office each team member has their own profile, replacing traditional diaries completely. What really stands out for me is that it’s free and works seamlessly across laptops and phones, which makes it incredibly practical to add tasks on the go and tick them off once they’re done (very satisfying!). It’s simple, , and genuinely makes life more organised.

So far I love this app even though it's free it's the best so far!! They have a few free customization and widgets. They have three views for tasks list such as List View, Kanban View and Timeline View (Premium). I like how they also add achievement and progress, repeated tasks /habit, calendar view and as for constant reminder it's paid so you need to set up a few reminder manually for free version. They have priority tags (4 levels) and somehow they also have Pomodoro and Stopwatch.

Best reminder App. The free portion does more than any other free app PLUS you can easily customize. It helps keep my calendar from getting cluttered so I can see appointments & events easier. I use it more & more.

I tried a lot of to-do list apps and this was the one that worked the best for me. Very well done and easy to use interface. I went ahead and signed up for the premium service even though I don't really need it just to help keep these guys in business. :)

absolutely mind blowing coming from Microsoft to do, premium version looks awesome but even the free version is leagues better than MTD. so much customisation and organization; I'm IN LOVE. 💗💗 bug: so so laggy after new update, regret updating :( tasks are disappearing now and can only find them when i search

Beast of an app. An all-in-one app for me. I am using it for to-do tasks, daily habits, and daily journaling. I had been searching for an all-in-one app for a very long time. So glad I decided to go and explore deeply with TickTick. This is my daily app now 😇
not as intuitive. like how do I add a habit. when you click the plus sign its only for tasks. I like it but it needs work to.be a bit more user friendly. it feels a bit overwhelming
Using it for almost 10 years. Tried every todo app but this just sticks. Really hope for more lighter design overall. Also more options and designs for widgets would be great really. Recommend.
